publicationstmt.publisher: Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections

profiledesc.creation: This finding aid was produced using ArchivesSpace on 2023-09-12 00:05:38 UTC.
profiledesc.langusage: Description is written in: Undetermined, Code for undetermined script script.

change.item: This resource was modified by the ArchivesSpace Preprocessor developed by the Harvard Library (https://github.com/harvard-library/archivesspace-preprocessor)

repository.corpname: Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections

did.abstract: Material documenting the shows and operations of the Cornell Concert Commission mostly from 1971-1992, includes tour riders, posters, advertising material, and scrapbooks.

defitem.label: Contact Information:
defitem.item: Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections 2B Carl A. Kroch Library Cornell University Ithaca, NY 14853 (607) 255-3530 Fax: (607) 255-9524rareref@cornell.eduhttp://rmc.library.cornell.edu

scopecontent.head: COLLECTION DESCRIPTION
scopecontent.p: Material documenting the shows and operations of the Cornell Concert Commission mostly from 1971-1992, includes tour riders, posters, advertising material, and scrapbooks.

prefercite.head: Cite As:
prefercite.p: Cornell Concert Commission records #37-7-3832. Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections, Cornell University Library.

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: Boxes 4 (unprocessed) through Box 13 (unprocessed) are restricted to permission of the office of origin.

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: Boxes 4 (unprocessed) through Box 13 (unprocessed) are restricted until processed.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes tour riders, publicity material, correspondence, financial records, crew lists, newspaper clippings, and show advertisments

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Tour rider includes clause emphasizing no all white audiences

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes notes on incidents during show

did.unittitle: Paul Simon; Urubama; Jesse Dixon Singers
Sly & the Family Stone; the Wailers

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Press release regarding show cancellation, mentions financial settlement discussions from Deep Purple show

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Cancelled

did.unittitle: Creative Source; Voices of East Harlem; New Birth

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Miles Davis cancelled

did.unittitle: One Flew Over the Cuckoo's Nest
New Riders of the Purple Sage

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Tour rider includes suggestion to hire "two ladies" to serve as hostesses

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes note about "the ridiculous audience control pattern"

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Tour rider includes detailed food requests

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Cancelled

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Tour rider includes request for 500 lbs. of dry ice

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Tour rider includes request for 350 lbs. of dry ice; emphasis placed on organic foods

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes concert t-shirt sketches

did.unittitle: Southside Johnny and the Asbury Jukes

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Wet Willie; Stanky Brown

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Cancelled

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes flyers advertising WVBR's Romeo and Juliet contest to pair up strangers for dinner and the concert

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Tour rider includes clause refusing to perform before audiences segregated on the basis of race, color, creed or sex

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes memo detailing injuries from this concert and Kinks show

did.unittitle: Echo & the Bunnymen; the Leather Nun

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Tour rider prohibits "Slam Dancing and Stage Diving".

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Student Assembly commends Cornell Concert Commission, claiming "the group stirred up more excitement on campus than any group has in years… you have quieted, for a time, the many students who clamor for big-name bands to come on campus". Enclosed is a form detailing a poster violation filed by the Robert Purcell Union (along with copy of offending poster).

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Tracy Chapman had "just concluded performing with the Amnesty International Tour all over the world, and [had] scheduled only five shoes in the Western Hemisphere, one of which is at Cornell".

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Contract prohibits the presence of a piano "in the artist's suite".

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes ballot for election of Cornell Concert Commission Executive Board.

did.unittitle: Edie Bricknell & New Bohemians; Steve Forbert

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Concert was co-presented by the Third World Student Programming Board.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Performers: the Toasters; the Feelies; Includes two 7" vinyl records featuring The Toaster's "Talk Is Cheap", and a letter discussing the possibility of hosting a summer venue at Schoelkopf Field.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Artist specifically agreed to perform, "An Evening With" (see additional provisions in contract).

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Performers: Third World; Soul Jahs; bop (harvey)

did.unittitle: The Alarm; The Kevin McDermott Orchestra

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes letter detailing a theft that took place after the concert, and very detailed advertising routes for Cornell Concert Commission members.

did.unittitle: Robert Fripp & The League of Crafty Guitarists

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes letter describing artist's appreciation for Cornell University's excellent hospitality and cetering services, and index cards with reviews of the concert

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes information regarding the MTV Save the Earth Campaign; stickers from the event, a copy of the April edition of MTV To Go, along with an essay of sorts titled "CIJ on being a woman in the music industry" .

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Performer: NBRQ, Cancelled

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: The Joe Ely tour rider explicitly demands "no insects or animal organs - please!"

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Cancelled

did.unittitle: Timothy Wright (and the Concert Choir); The Wade Sisters, Lorraine Stancil
Boogie Down Productions; Ed O.G.; Da Bulldogs

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Commercial thirty seconds in length was broadcasted on MTV and ESPN sometime between 6 AM and midnight

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Original date of concert April 23, 1991 was cancelled, reschedule form does not indicate new date

did.unittitle: Bernie Milton and the Soul Patrol

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Involved collaboration between Cornell Concert Commission and the Festival of Black Gospel Committee; cancelled because record label did not respond in time. Includes resume of William David Stewart, a gospel singer. Enclosed is a letter from the Alpha Kappa Alpha Sorority requesting funds to host "The Boy's Choir of Harlem".

did.unittitle: Naughty by Nature, Queen Latifah, and Blacksheep

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: The Flavor Unit Tour

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Performers: Violent Femmes, Fishbone, Blue yster Cult, and Tribe. Transcripts of WJPZ Syracuse radio broadcasts of this event are included.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes an investigation report detailing two incidents involving minors; "the seventeen year old has a possible broken ankle… [and] the sixteen year old was kicked in the head…"

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes Elton John. Jason, Blood Sweat and Tears, Beach Boys, The Byrds, Herbie Hancock, Miles Davis, Bobby Byrd and the JB's, Mahavishnu Orchestra, Loggins and Messina, Deep Purple has information about the riot during the show.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes various acts, some clippings for concerts from performers who came to Cornell, but not necessarily for their Cornell performance.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes Joni Mitchell, Yes, Four Tops, Jefferson Starship, The Mahavishnu Orchestra and Weather Report, Judy Collins, Paul Simon, Sly and the Family Stone, David Crosby and Graham Nash, The Band with Aztec Two-Step, Santana, Taj Mahal with Minnie Ripperton, Return to Forever with Chick Corea, The Eleventh House with Larry Coryell, Billy Joel.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes Linda Ronstadt, Renaissance, New Riders of the Purple Sage, Grateful Dead.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes Stanley Jordan, Lady Smith Black Mambazo with Samite, Edie Brickell and New Bohemians, Tito Puente and De La Soul.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Poster design is a silk screen copy of a North Vietnamese postage stamp portraying a woman warrior.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Count: 3

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Count: 3

did.unittitle: Elton John, David Johnstone, Dee Murray, Nigel Olsson and Family
The Mahavishnu Orchestra with John McLaughlin

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Count: 2

did.unittitle: Deep Purple, Billy Preston and Family

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Freshman Week Concert

did.unittitle: Paul Simon, Urubamba, Jesse Dixson Singers

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Count: 2

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Count: 2

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Count: 4

did.unittitle: Joni Mitchell, Tom Scott, L.A. Express

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Count: 4

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Count: 12

did.unittitle: Four Tops, the Voices of East Harlem

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Count: 6

did.unittitle: Jefferson Starship, John Barbata, Papa John Creach, Peter Kangaroo, David Freiberg, Craig Chaquiso, Grace Slick, Paul Kantner

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Count: 8

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Count: 5

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: The New Birth, Creative Source, The Voices of East Harlem, Leon Russell, The Fab Furniture of Martin Mull; Count: 4

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Count: 3

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Count: 4

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Count: 4

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Return to Forever featuring Chick Corea, the Eleventh House featuring Larry Coryell, Michael Urbaniak and Fusion; Count: 3

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Count: 4

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Count: 3

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Jim Rooney and Partners in Crime, Johnny Shines, Tracy Nelson and Mother Earth, Bryan Bowers, Arlo Guthrie, David Amram, Jay and Lyn Ungar, Furry Lewis, Tom Paxton, Vassar Clements, Loudon Wainwright III; Count: 2

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Count: 2

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Count: 2

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Count: 2

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Southside Johnny & the Asbury Jukes; Count: 2

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Count: 2

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Count: 2

did.unittitle: Edie Brickell & New Bohemians, Steve Forbert

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Accounts written in 2016 describe May 8, 1977 concert

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Reprint photos of May 8, 1977 concert at Barton Hall

did.unittitle: Cornell Concert Commission backstage pass - Doobie Brothers
Cornell Concert Commission backstage pass - Joe Jackson
Cornell Concert Commission backstage pass - Marshall Tucker Band
Cornell Concert Commission backstage pass - Renaissance
Cornell Concert Commission backstage pass - Robert Palmer/Robert Hunter
Cornell Concert Commission backstage pass - Spring Festival
Cornell Concert Commission backstage pass - Spring Festival
Cornell Concert Commission backstage pass - The Cars
Cornell Concert Commission backstage pass - The Police
Cornell Concert Commission backstage pass - Warren Zevon
Monarch Entertainment backstage pass - Linda Ronstadt/Danny O'Keefe
Monarch Entertainment backstage pass - Bruce Springsteen
Cornell Concert Commission backstage pass - Grateful Dead
Sticker - The Kinks with Steve Forbert
